quizzes.title: Commercial Floor Care for Schools & Childcare — Knowledge Check

questions.questionText: You decant a floor stripper from its drum into a spray bottle for the day's work. Under the model WHS Regulations, what must you do?
options: Nothing — only the original drum needs a label
Correctly label the spray bottle (the decanted container) as well
Add it to a manifest before it can be used on site
Dispose of it the same day so no label is needed

questions.questionText: What is the best practice for cleaning responsibly in an occupied, child-occupied room?
options: Use the strongest chemistry available to be sure surfaces are clean
Skip reading the SDS to save time, since the product name tells you enough
Select lower-tox products, read each SDS, dilute per the label, ventilate, and isolate wet or treated areas from children
Reserve gentle products for periodic deep cleans and use caustic strippers for the daily routine

questions.questionText: Compared with encapsulation, hot water extraction (HWE) is generally associated with:
options: Deeper soil removal but more moisture and a longer return to service
The lowest moisture and the fastest return to service
No need to vacuum the carpet beforehand
A guaranteed fixed dry time you can quote the client

questions.questionText: When cleaning carpet in a child-occupied classroom or care room, which is the better practice?
options: Quote the client a fixed dry time so they can plan the day
Leave cords running across walkways so you can reach the far wall
Isolate and sign damp floors, manage cords, and sequence rooms so each dries and reopens while you move on
Clean every room at once so the whole job finishes quickly

questions.questionText: Why do grout lines tend to look dingy even when the tiles themselves look clean?
options: Grout is always manufactured in a darker colour than tile
Tiles are never sealed and grout always is
Grout is porous and traps soil below the surface, while the denser tile face rinses clean
Mopping cleans grout more effectively than it cleans tile

questions.questionText: What is the correct order for a sound tile and grout cleaning method?
options: Recover, rinse, agitate, dwell, apply, pre-wet
Pre-wet, apply, dwell, agitate, rinse, recover
Apply, recover, pre-wet, rinse, dwell, agitate
Agitate, apply, dwell, pre-wet, recover, rinse

questions.questionText: In the generic vinyl strip-and-seal process, what is the correct order of stages?
options: Apply finish, then strip, then rinse and neutralise
Strip the old finish, rinse and neutralise, dry, then apply multiple thin sealer and finish coats with drying between each coat
Dry the floor, strip, apply one thick coat, then reopen straight away
Neutralise, apply finish, strip, then rinse

questions.questionText: A freshly stripped and sealed floor is an existing, in-service surface that must be re-verified for slip resistance. Which statement is correct?
options: AS 4663 measures the finished in-service floor's slip resistance but does not set cure or re-entry times
AS 4663 tells you exactly when the floor is safe to reopen to children
AS 4586 is the right basis because the floor has a new finish on it
The sealer's SDS sets the required slip-resistance class for the site

questions.questionText: You are pressure washing a sand-jointed paver path at a childcare centre. Which approach best avoids surface damage?
options: Use the narrowest jet at maximum pressure to finish faster
Use the widest practical fan, keep the nozzle moving, and test a small out-of-the-way area first
Hold a tight jet on each joint until it is fully cleared
Increase the pressure until the jointing sand washes out of the joints

questions.questionText: How should pressure-washing run-off be handled on a school or childcare site?
options: Hosed straight into the nearest stormwater drain once the job is done
Left to evaporate off the surface on its own
Confined and collected, then directed to sewer under a trade-waste agreement with the water authority
Tipped into a nearby garden bed away from the play area

questions.questionText: Periodic work such as hard-floor strip and seal or carpet hot water extraction is typically scheduled into:
options: The daily routine clean
Peak occupancy, so the client can see the work being done
Whenever a worker happens to have a spare hour
Deep-clean or school-holiday windows, then verified against the current contract spec

questions.questionText: Working With Children checks for contractors at schools and childcare centres are:
options: A single national check that is valid in every state and territory
Issued only to permanent school staff and never to contractors
State and territory based, generally not auto-transferable, and not necessarily the only screening a site requires
Fully replaced by an ordinary police check
